mnemonics: PACT: People Are Core to Testing. This reminds you that the four key elements to test for are People, Activities, Contexts, and Technologies.
TEST DRIVE: Test Early, Simulate Thoroughly, Design Realistic Interactions, Validate Everything. This emphasizes starting tests early and using realistic scenarios.
USER FOCUS: Use Scenarios, Evaluate Regularly, Feedback Often, Observe Closely, User-centered, Simulate Realism. This reinforces the core principles of human-centered testing.
keyPoints: The goal is to evaluate how well the AI system supports human needs, capabilities, and expectations.
Testing should involve realistic scenarios and tasks performed by representative users.
Evaluation must consider the entire user experience, including trust, fairness, and potential for harm.
Testing is not a single phase but an iterative process integrated throughout the development lifecycle.
Both quantitative metrics (e.g., task success rate) and qualitative feedback (e.g., user satisfaction) are crucial.
commonMistakes: Testing too late in the development process when changes are costly to implement.
Using developers or other non-representative users as test participants, which skews results.
Focusing only on technical performance (e.g., accuracy, speed) and ignoring human factors (e.g., trust, usability).
Designing artificial test tasks that do not reflect how the system will be used in a real-world context.
Failing to test for safety, bias, and potential misuse across diverse user groups and edge cases.
